cards.back: Border Gateway Protocol — path-vector routing protocol used between Autonomous Systems (ASes). Backbone of the internet. Slow to converge but extremely scalable + policy-rich.

cards.front: Border Gateway Protocol — path-vector routing protocol used between Autonomous Systems (ASes). Backbone of the internet. Slow to converge but extremely scalable + policy-rich.
BGP transport protocol + port: [...] port 179. Unlike IGPs (OSPF/EIGRP) that use their own L3 protocol numbers, BGP rides TCP for reliability.
BGP transport protocol + port: [...] port 179. Unlike IGPs (OSPF/EIGRP) that use their own L3 protocol numbers, BGP rides TCP for reliability.
What does BGP carry that IGPs don't?
cards.back: **AS path** — full list of autonomous systems a route traversed. Used for loop prevention (if local AS in path, drop) and policy decisions.

- **eBGP** — between routers in different ASes. AD = 20.
- **iBGP** — between routers in same AS. AD = 200. Typically peer over loopbacks; full-mesh or route reflectors.

cards.front: When would you use BGP instead of OSPF?
cards.back:
- **Inter-AS routing** (between organizations / ISPs)
- **Internet routing tables** (~1M+ prefixes)
- **Policy-rich routing** (preferring certain providers, traffic engineering)
- OSPF is for INTRA-AS (within one company), BGP for INTER-AS.

cards.front:
eBGP default Administrative Distance: [...] (lower than OSPF 110 — eBGP wins by default).
iBGP default Administrative Distance: 200 (higher than most IGPs — IGP wins by default).

eBGP default Administrative Distance: [...] (lower than OSPF 110 — eBGP wins by default).
iBGP default Administrative Distance: 200 (higher than most IGPs — IGP wins by default).
BGP is what type of routing protocol?
cards.back: **Path-vector**. Not distance-vector (RIP), not link-state (OSPF/IS-IS). Path-vector carries full AS_PATH and uses it for loop prevention + policy.

- 2-byte ASN: 1-65535 (1-64495 public, 64512-65534 private)
- 4-byte ASN: 1-4294967295 (modern; required as 2-byte space exhausted)

cards.front: Why does CW use BGP heavily even within a single data center?
cards.back: Modern DC fabrics (spine-leaf) use BGP-EVPN for VXLAN overlay. eBGP between leaf and spine. Out of CCNA scope but relevant for CW EVI01 work.
